description: Trigger and behavior cases for data/model poisoning: contributor-trust assessment, poisoning paths, feedback-loop Sybil defense, ingestion-as-truth integrity, provenance/holdout controls, boundary with supply chain.

cases.prompt: We retrain our model weekly on the conversations that users rated thumbs-up. Review this for poisoning risk.

assertions: Identifies the open feedback loop as attacker-reachable and traces a poisoning path (coordinated thumbs-up steers the next update).
Requires identity/rate controls on feedback, anomaly detection, and human review before feedback becomes training data.
Requires a holdout/canary and adversarial-trigger evaluation that would catch a behavior shift before shipping, noting aggregate accuracy won't reveal a targeted backdoor.

cases.prompt: Our knowledge base ingests public web pages and the assistant answers from them as authoritative. Any poisoning concern?

assertions: Treats indexing external content as ground truth as a poisoning vector: an attacker who gets a document indexed can steer answers for a target query.
Requires source trust classification, validation before indexing, and the ability to purge AND roll back poisoned content.
Distinguishes this integrity concern from retrieval authorization (rag-security-architect) and hands any injection payload to prompt-injection-defender.

cases.prompt: We're about to download a community fine-tuning adapter for our base model from a public hub. Is it safe to use?

assertions: Assessing an acquired third-party model/dataset/adapter is supply-chain-security-reviewer (LLM03, extended); this skill covers data you ingest and pipelines you run, not artifacts you download.

cases.prompt: To move fast, just automatically fold all user corrections straight into the next fine-tune with no review — trust the users.

assertions: Refuses auto-training on raw open feedback: it lets a coordinated group vote bias or a backdoor into the model.
Requires human review, identity/rate limits, and anomaly detection before feedback shapes behavior.
Notes remediation of any resulting corruption (retrain/purge/rollback) is a classified, approved step via human-approval-boundary.
